Title: Increase in lysophosphatidate acyltransferase activity in oilseed rape (Brassica napus) increases seed triacylglycerol content despite its low intrinsic flux control coefficient

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acylates lysophosphatidic acid (1-acyl-sn-glycerol-3-phosphate) with acyl-CoA to form phosphatidic acid in the second step of the Kennedy pathway for triacylglycerol synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Lysophosphatidic acid; oleoyl-CoA (and reported background characterization includes 16:0-CoA and 18:1-CoA)
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream-midstream; second of four reactions in the Kennedy pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an intermediate precursor (phosphatidic acid) required for downstream TAG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as sole rate-limiting step; supported as a significant flux-control point in lipid assembly with in planta flux control coefficient c. 0.14-0.17 in controls
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major seed storage oil; agricultural vegetable oil
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased seed TAG content by 25-29% per seed and altered fatty acid distribution in PA, PC, DAG and TAG.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Expression of Tropaeolum majus LPAAT in Brassica napus DH12075 increased microsomal LPAAT activity about 1.5-2.0-fold in developing embryos. 2. Increased LPAAT activity raised mature seed TAG content by 25-29% per seed. 3. In planta flux control coefficient of LPAAT over TAG accumulation was estimated at ~0.10-0.14 by small-change analysis and ~0.14-0.17 in controls by large-change correction, indicating meaningful but distributed control. 4. LPAAT overexpression changed glycerolipid labeling and acyl composition, especially in PA, PC, DAG and TAG.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in Brassica napus; microsomal enzyme assay; seed TAG quantification; lipid class/fatty acid profiling; [U-14C]glycerol radiolabelling; flux control analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured in developing embryos at 27 days after flowering during rapid TAG accumulation phase
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed development stage (27 DAF); exogenous oleate; diazepam
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No reduction in seed TAG percentage was observed; increased TAG per seed was associated with increased seed size. Other yield or stress tradeoffs not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for increasing seed oil content in oilseed rape; likely higher value when stacked with DGAT to further increase TAG synthesis; seed-specific expression is appropriate.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Construct referred to as nasturtium LPAAT / NastLPAT. Kinetic parameters measured in microsomes: Vmax 9.8 nmol min-1 mg-1, Km 103.1 uM for oleoyl-CoA and 15.2 uM for LPA. The study emphasizes that small intrinsic flux control can still yield substantial storage-product accumulation because TAG accumulation is exponential.
